preview_text:
The model favors France at 55% to win, with Norway's probability of victory standing at 17%. France enters as a clear favorite, though the 37-percentage-point gap leaves meaningful room for an upset or a stalemate result.

The draw carries 28% probability in the model's forecast, making it the second-likeliest outcome. This relatively high draw likelihood reflects a match expected to be competitive rather than one-sided. The spread across all three outcomes indicates a moderately open contest where France holds genuine advantage but neither side is heavily favored to dominate play decisively.

The sportsbook consensus aligns closely with the model across all three outcomes. The market assigns France 52% (versus the model's 55%), Norway 22% (versus 17%), and a draw 25% (versus 28%). The gaps are all under 5 percentage points, suggesting broad agreement on the match's shape. Polymarket prices similarly cluster near the model and book estimates, with France at 54%.
